coreIssue: This junior Brand Designer performance conversation is about making the quality bar concrete enough for Carlos to apply in his day-to-day work.
openingQuestion: What does strong junior-level brand design work look like to you right now, and where does that feel hardest to apply in your recent projects?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent design, review, or revision cycle instead of speaking only in generalities
whether prior feedback shows up as a concrete change in how he briefs, explores, presents, or revises work
if they struggle to describe the junior-level bar without leaning on your approval as the main signal
avoid: do not turn the meeting into a vague critique of taste without examples from his actual brand design work
do not overload a junior designer with multiple improvement themes when one usable practice is enough for this conversation
goodOutcome: You and Carlos have agreed what good junior Brand Designer work looks like in one or two concrete dimensions, plus one immediate practice he will use in upcoming design iterations.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick two recent pieces of Carlos's work—one stronger and one weaker—and note the specific design choices that show the current quality bar.
